items.title: Qwen is turning language into world models
items.oneliner: Qwen-AgentWorld scales to 397B parameters and 10 million trajectories, pushing LLMs closer to trainable simulators for agents.

items.title: Krea is competing on controllable image generation
items.oneliner: Krea 2 bets image generation wins on control and aesthetic range, not just benchmark scores or default pretty outputs.

items.title: AI biology gets its own language
items.oneliner: Arc Institute's Proto gives AI biology pipelines a native programming model, which could make model chaining reproducible instead of fragile.

items.title: Claude is becoming a Slack coworker
items.oneliner: Anthropic's Claude Tag moves agents from chat windows into team workflows, where permissions and async delegation suddenly matter.

items.title: Prompt injection is really role confusion
items.oneliner: Simon Willison's framing is useful: prompt injection persists because models cannot reliably separate instructions, data, and authority.

items.title: LLMs changed vulnerability reporting economics
items.oneliner: Filippo argues vuln reports are no longer rare expertise, so maintainers need faster triage systems, not old disclosure rituals.

items.title: Quantum deadlines are now federal policy
items.oneliner: Two US quantum orders put post-quantum migration on a real clock, making crypto inventory and upgrade plans board-level work.

items.title: OpenAI is moving deeper into cyber defense
items.oneliner: IBM joining OpenAI's Daybreak program signals frontier models are becoming part of enterprise vulnerability discovery pipelines.

items.title: Groq raised another infrastructure war chest
items.oneliner: Groq's $650 million raise says inference demand is still outrunning supply, and specialized serving hardware remains investable.

items.title: Apple just absorbed Swift Package Index
items.oneliner: Apple buying Swift Package Index tightens the Swift toolchain loop and gives package discovery first-party leverage.
